title: crotalaria lunulata
intro: Crotalaria lunulata, also known as moonseed or bush calabar, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and crescent-shaped seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Crotalaria lunulata include treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as reducing fever and relieving pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often used to make an infusion or decoction, and it can also be used as a paste or cream to apply topically.
